pr.title: Match vi0102, vi0801 & link, work on vi0501 and vi1202
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Matched the visual/cutscene objects vi0102.c and vi0801.c, updated build configuration to mark both as Matching, and adjusted GALE01 splits/symbols to reflect new section ownership, symbol scopes, and newly identified vi0801 names. The PR also made matching-oriented cleanup in vi0501.c and vi1202.c, fixed related scene-handler prototypes/usages in gmscdata.c, and corrected a Big Blue route prototype from char* to HSD_JObj**.
what_changed: configure.py changed melee/vi/vi0102.c and melee/vi/vi0801.c from NonMatching to Matching in the vi visual/cutscene object list.

config/GALE01/splits.txt moved bss ownership around the visual files: textlib bss shrank, vi0801 gained bss at 0x804A2EA8-0x804A2F08, and vi1202 gained bss at 0x804A2F08-0x804A2F38. vi0102 sbss was extended to end at 0x804D6F3C.

config/GALE01/symbols.txt renamed previously unknown vi0801 functions from un_/fn_ names to vi0801-specific names, including vi0801_8031ED70, vi0801_8031EE60, vi0801_8031EE84, vi0801_8031EFE4, vi0801_OnEnter, and vi0801_OnFrame.

config/GALE01/symbols.txt tightened symbol scopes for matched local code/data, such as vi0102_RunFrame becoming local, erase_colors_vi0102 becoming local, several vi0102/vi0801 float constants becoming local @ labels, and local data strings replacing global un_ names.
src/melee/gm/gmscdata.c updated minor-scene handler entries to call vi0102_Initialize_OnEnter without a cast and to use the newly named vi0801_OnFrame and vi0801_OnEnter for MN_CUTSCENE_FZERO.

src/melee/vi/vi0102.c changed vi0102_Initialize_OnEnter from taking ViCharaDesc* directly to taking void* and casting internally, matching the generic minor-scene on-enter callback style. The matching code also introduced a small unused static f32 helper explicitly noted as forcing float ordering.
src/melee/vi/vi0102.h updated the vi0102_Initialize_OnEnter prototype to void*.

src/melee/vi/vi0801.c replaced ad-hoc externs and a local vi_GObj surrogate with concrete definitions/types visible in the diff: HSD_JObj* un_804A2EA8[24], initialized s32 un_80400128[23][2], and HSD_GObj field usage such as gxlink_prios.
src/melee/vi/vi0801.c renamed internal functions from unknown names toward vi0801-prefixed identifiers and removed an lb/lb_00B0.h include that was no longer needed.

src/melee/vi/vi0501.c received matching-oriented variable-ordering and expression adjustments, including moving the void* cast to the top, changing 0.0f to 0.0F, introducing temporary HSD_JObj* variables before GObj object setup, and using deliberately equivalent expressions such as `(model != NULL) * 0` and `(int) (&desc->spawn_count)`.
src/melee/gr/grbigblueroute.h corrected grBigBlueRoute_8020DAB4's first parameter type from char* to HSD_JObj**.

src/melee/vi/vi1202.c and src/melee/vi/vi0801.h were changed, but the provided excerpt does not show enough detail to summarize their exact edits beyond the PR title's statement that vi1202 was worked on and vi0801's interface was adjusted.
smart_moves: Used configuration, split, and symbol updates together with source changes so the newly matched vi0102 and vi0801 objects had consistent build status and section ownership.
Promoted unknown vi0801 function names to file-scoped semantic names only where addresses and usage supported it, especially the on-enter/on-frame callbacks wired through gmscdata.c.
Converted on-enter callbacks to generic void* signatures where the scene-handler table expects that shape, avoiding casts at call sites and better matching callback infrastructure.
Replaced a hand-rolled GObj overlay struct in vi0801 with HSD_GObj field access, which reduces local fake typing and makes future matching work more reusable.
Defined the vi0801 bss array and data table in C rather than leaving them as extern unknowns, which helped establish correct section placement and data layout.

Inserted a documented unused static f32-returning function in vi0102 specifically to force float ordering, a practical matching tactic when the compiler/linker orders constants or functions differently.
Used temporary JObj pointer aliases and intentionally redundant arithmetic/casts to influence code generation without changing runtime behavior.
decomp_lessons: When moving an object from NonMatching to Matching, update configure.py, splits.txt, symbols.txt, source, and headers as one unit; matching status depends on all of these staying synchronized.
Scene on-enter handlers in minor-scene tables should generally use the generic `void*` callback ABI and cast to the real descriptor type inside the function.
Local static functions, local string labels, and local float constants should be marked local in symbols.txt once the source owns them; leaving them global can obscure true object boundaries.
BSS boundaries may need to move between adjacent objects once previously unknown arrays are defined in the correct C file.
A fake struct used only to reach known fields can often be removed once the canonical engine struct has the relevant fields named, improving both match quality and maintainability.
Matching may require source-level no-op transformations: temporary variables, equivalent zero expressions, uppercase float suffixes, and cast placement can all affect generated code.
Prototype fixes in unrelated-looking headers can be necessary for matching nearby visual/cutscene code if the cutscene calls into stage-specific APIs.
naming_conventions: Use the vi file prefix for visual/cutscene symbols once they are understood, e.g. `vi0801_OnEnter`, `vi0801_OnFrame`, and `vi0801_8031ED70` instead of generic `un_8031F07C_OnEnter` or `fn_8031EFE4`.
Keep address-bearing names for functions whose purpose is still unknown, but replace `un_`/`fn_` with the owning module prefix when file ownership is clear.
Use `*_OnEnter` and `*_OnFrame` suffixes for minor-scene callbacks wired through `MinorSceneHandler` entries.
Use local compiler-style `@NNN` labels in symbols.txt for local strings and constants rather than global `un_` labels when the data is only file-local.
Erase-color globals follow the pattern `erase_colors_viXXXX`, though this PR made `erase_colors_vi0102` local in symbols.txt once source ownership was established.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Mark matched objects in configure.py only after their source, symbols, and section splits line up.
Add a small unused static function returning `0.0f` to force float ordering within an object; the vi0102 source explicitly comments this tactic.
Adjust declaration order inside functions to influence register allocation and stack layout.
Introduce temporary pointer aliases such as assigning `jobj` into another `HSD_JObj*` before passing it to selected functions to alter generated code shape.
Use semantically no-op expressions like `(ptr != NULL) * 0` to force the compiler into the desired instruction pattern while still passing zero.
Use exact float literal spelling such as `0.0F` where it affects matching or consistency.
Prefer real engine struct fields, such as `HSD_GObj.gxlink_prios`, over local byte-pad surrogate structs when the canonical struct is known.
Split aggregate data in symbols.txt into correctly typed strings, arrays, bss, sbss, and local constants so the linker places following objects correctly.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context, and the human text excerpt is empty. There is one issue comment counted, but its body was not included, so no concrete reviewer feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Inspect the full diff for src/melee/vi/vi0801.c to capture all renamed functions and matching tricks not visible in the truncated excerpt.
Inspect src/melee/vi/vi0801.h to determine the exact header/API changes paired with the vi0801 match.
Inspect src/melee/vi/vi1202.c to identify what work was done there and whether it involved bss ownership, string splitting, or callback prototypes.
Check the omitted issue comment for any maintainer note or merge-bot result that could add review context.
Search future PRs for reuse of the `force float ordering` unused static f32 tactic.
